[Folk rock](/wiki/Folk_rock "Folk rock"), [Psychedelic rock](/wiki/Psychedelic_rock "Psychedelic rock"), [Pagan rock](/wiki/Pagan_rock "Pagan rock")

[Christopher Bingham](/w/index.php?title=Christopher_Bingham_(musician)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Christopher Bingham (musician) (page does not exist)"), [Sue Tinney](/w/index.php?title=Sue_Tinney&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sue Tinney (page does not exist)"), [Larry Golding](/w/index.php?title=Larry_Golding&action=edit&redlink=1 "Larry Golding (page does not exist)"), [Sunnie Reed](/w/index.php?title=Sunnie_Reed&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sunnie Reed (page does not exist)"), [T.J. Morris (page does not exist)"), [Jay Kenney](/w/index.php?title=Jay_Kenney&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jay Kenney (page does not exist)"), [Betsy Tinney](/w/index.php?title=Betsy_Tinney&action=edit&redlink=1 "Betsy Tinney (page does not exist)"), [Dan Mohler](/w/index.php?title=Dan_Mohler&action=edit&redlink=1 "Dan Mohler (page does not exist)")

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Gaia+Consort%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Gaia+Consort%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Gaia+Consort%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Gaia+Consort%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(January 2010)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

is the original incarnation of [Seattle](/wiki/Seattle,_Washington "Seattle, Washington")-based [folk rock](/wiki/Folk-rock "Folk-rock") music-group [Bone Poets Orchestra](/w/index.php?title=Bone_Poets_Orchestra&action=edit&redlink=1 "Bone Poets Orchestra (page does not exist)").[[1]](#cite_note-gaia_consort-1) The group, popular in the Pacific Northwest of the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"), was founded as Gaia Consort by [Christopher Bingham](/w/index.php?title=Christopher_Bingham_(musician)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Christopher Bingham (musician) (page does not exist)") and [Sue Tinney](/w/index.php?title=Sue_Tinney&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sue Tinney (page does not exist)") in 1997. Their first performance was at Nudestock 97. Gaia Consort has worked with a large number of musicians from the Pacific NorthWest, including [Heather Alexander](/wiki/Heather_Alexander "Heather Alexander") and [Dan Ochipinti](/w/index.php?title=Dan_Ochipinti&action=edit&redlink=1 "Dan Ochipinti (page does not exist)") of the Celtic folk-rock band [Uffington Horse](/wiki/Uffington_Horse "Uffington Horse").

["Interview with Gaia Consort from New Witch Magazine, issue 7, 2004"](https://web.archive.org/web/20060507055445/http://www.newwitch.com/archives/07/readnewwitch/gaiaconsort.html). Archived from [the original](http://www.newwitch.com/archives/07/readnewwitch/gaiaconsort.html) on May 7, 2006. Retrieved April 19, 2006.

first_paragraph: **Gaia Consort** is the original incarnation of [Seattle](/wiki/Seattle,_Washington "Seattle, Washington")-based [folk rock](/wiki/Folk-rock "Folk-rock") music-group [Bone Poets Orchestra](/w/index.php?title=Bone_Poets_Orchestra&action=edit&redlink=1 "Bone Poets Orchestra (page does not exist)").[[1]](#cite_note-gaia_consort-1) The group, popular in the Pacific Northwest of the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"), was founded as Gaia Consort by [Christopher Bingham](/w/index.php?title=Christopher_Bingham_(musician)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Christopher Bingham (musician) (page does not exist)") and [Sue Tinney](/w/index.php?title=Sue_Tinney&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sue Tinney (page does not exist)") in 1997. Their first performance was at Nudestock 97. Gaia Consort has worked with a large number of musicians from the Pacific NorthWest, including [Heather Alexander](/wiki/Heather_Alexander "Heather Alexander") and [Dan Ochipinti](/w/index.php?title=Dan_Ochipinti&action=edit&redlink=1 "Dan Ochipinti (page does not exist)") of the Celtic folk-rock band [Uffington Horse](/wiki/Uffington_Horse "Uffington Horse").

**Gaia Consort** is the original incarnation of [Seattle](/wiki/Seattle,_Washington "Seattle, Washington")-based [folk rock](/wiki/Folk-rock "Folk-rock") music-group [Bone Poets Orchestra](/w/index.php?title=Bone_Poets_Orchestra&action=edit&redlink=1 "Bone Poets Orchestra (page does not exist)").[[1]](#cite_note-gaia_consort-1) The group, popular in the Pacific Northwest of the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"), was founded as Gaia Consort by [Christopher Bingham](/w/index.php?title=Christopher_Bingham_(musician)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Christopher Bingham (musician) (page does not exist)") and [Sue Tinney](/w/index.php?title=Sue_Tinney&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sue Tinney (page does not exist)") in 1997. Their first performance was at Nudestock 97. Gaia Consort has worked with a large number of musicians from the Pacific NorthWest, including [Heather Alexander](/wiki/Heather_Alexander "Heather Alexander") and [Dan Ochipinti](/w/index.php?title=Dan_Ochipinti&action=edit&redlink=1 "Dan Ochipinti (page does not exist)") of the Celtic folk-rock band [Uffington Horse](/wiki/Uffington_Horse "Uffington Horse").

Gaia Consort released their first album, *Gaia Circles* in 1999, their second album *Secret Voices* in 2001, their third *Evolve* in 2004, and their fourth *Vitus Dance* in 2007.[[1]](#cite_note-gaia_consort-1)

The group has focused on playing at house concerts, self-produced shows and events such as [Faerieworlds](/wiki/Faerieworlds "Faerieworlds") and [Heartland Pagan Festival](/w/index.php?title=Heartland_Pagan_Festival&action=edit&redlink=1 "Heartland Pagan Festival (page does not exist)"), instead of more traditional musical venues. This, in addition to their songs which frequently involve themes such as [neopaganism](/wiki/Neopaganism "Neopaganism"), positive sexuality and [polyamory](/wiki/Polyamory "Polyamory"), has made Gaia Consort a favorite of the pagan and [sexual subcultures](/wiki/Sexual_subcultures "Sexual subcultures") of the Pacific Northwest.[[2]](#cite_note-interview-2) They enjoy a strong fan base, evidenced from the fact that all of their albums are financed by donations from the listening community. Seattle filmmaker and polyamory advocate [Terisa Greenan](/wiki/Terisa_Greenan "Terisa Greenan") has produced several music videos for Gaia Consort.

Gaia Consort's philosophy is somewhat unusual in the music industry: the majority of the band's music is available freely on their website, and Gaia Consort actively encourages the non-commercial sharing of these MP3-format songs, saying that 'music should not be bottled up' for the few who can afford to pay for it.

A major theme of much of Gaia Consort's work is the greater world and how it affects our lives; religion is one such target, while other songs have a decidedly 'anti-war' tone (one song asks 'What will it come to when we've finally had enough?').
